channel.title: Chart Breakers & Star Stories
channel.itunes:author: Family TVR
channel.itunes:summary: Did your favorite artist just break a record or a heart? The charts are screaming, and we have the inside story you won't hear anywhere else.
channel.description: The latest music trends, chart updates, and star stories from Billboard and beyond. Stay updated with the Pop Culture.

item.title: Nedra Talley Ross, The Ronettes’ Last Surviving Member, Dies at 80
item.description:
[Ethereal music fades out, and the host's voice takes over]
Host: Welcome to this special edition of our podcast, where we're paying tribute to a music legend. Nedra Talley Ross, the last surviving member of the iconic 1960s girl group The Ronettes, has passed away at the age of 80.
[Soft music plays in the background]
Host: According to a statement released on the group's social media accounts, Nedra's passing has left a void in the hearts of those who knew and loved her. As a founding member of The Ronettes, along with her cousins Ronnie and Estelle, Nedra's voice, style, and spirit helped define a sound that would change music forever.
[Clip of "Be My Baby" plays briefly]
Host: You might remember The Ronettes' hit song "Be My Baby," which peaked at number 2 on the Hot 100 in 1963. This influential track, co-written by Phil Spector, Jeff Barry, and Ellie Greenwich, remains one of the group's most iconic songs.
[Soft music continues to play]
Host: In a recent interview, just a couple of months before her passing, Nedra shared her start in music, crediting her mother and grandmother for inspiring her to sing. She also spoke about the strong family bond that kept The Ronettes together, both on and off stage.
[Clip of Nedra's interview plays]
Nedra Talley Ross: "We held each other's hand when we walked out on stage, and we believed that God was watching what we did."
Host: Nedra's legacy will live on through The Ronettes' timeless music and the impact they had on the music industry. We'll always remember her contribution to the group's story and their defining influence on pop music.
[Soft music swells, and the host's voice becomes more somber]
Host: Rest in peace, Nedra Talley Ross. Your music and spirit will be deeply missed, but your memory will live on forever. Thanks for the magic.
[Music fades out, and the host's voice takes over one last time]
Host: That's all for today's special tribute. Join us next time for more music stories and legends.
